reasoning_model_1: The model generates the sentence 'I've finished reading this report, no issue.' The requested text was 'I've finished reading this report, no issues.' There is a minor content discrepancy where the plural 'issues' is read as the singular 'issue' (detected around the end of the phrase). Regarding the target feature 'Volume', the audio maintains a consistent, normal conversational volume throughout the segment (0.0s - 3.5s). There is no whispering or shouting. However, due to the minor content omission (missing the final 's'), the model cannot receive a perfect score according to the guidelines, falling into the 'Partially correct' category for content accuracy.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the sentence 'I finish reading this report, no issues.' The requested text was 'I've finished reading this report, no issues.' The model fails to articulate 'I've finished', producing 'I finish' instead (0.0s - 0.8s). This constitutes a grammatical error or a tense deviation that affects the naturalness and accuracy of the script. Regarding the target feature 'Volume', the speech is delivered at a normal volume (0.0s - 3.5s), fulfilling the paralinguistic requirement. However, the content error ('I finish' vs 'I've finished') is a noticeable flaw that degrades the quality more significantly than a minor plural omission, placing it in the 'Major issues' category for content/fluency.
model_comparison: Both models successfully adhere to the 'Volume' constraint, speaking at a normal conversational level without shouting or whispering. The deciding factor is Content Accuracy. Model 1 (T1) has a very minor error, omitting the plural 's' in 'issues' ('no issue'), which is a subtle deviation. Model 2 (T2) commits a more significant error by changing the verb tense/phrase from 'I've finished' to 'I finish', which sounds grammatically incorrect and unnatural in this context. Therefore, Model 1 is superior due to better content preservation.
